title: Refactor with-multiple-sources where error tags come from two services
prompt:
Given:

```
def merged_profile(user_id) do
  case MyApp.Users.fetch(user_id) do
    {:ok, user} ->
      case MyApp.Analytics.get(user_id) do
        {:ok, stats} -> {:ok, Map.merge(user, %{stats: stats})}
        {:error, :analytics_down} -> {:ok, user}
        {:error, r} -> {:error, {:analytics, r}}
      end
    {:error, r} -> {:error, {:users, r}}
  end
end
```

The `{:error, :analytics_down}` branch gracefully falls back to the user alone. Rewrite as a `with` expression + a fallback match for analytics. Use `with {:ok, user} <- ..., {:ok, stats} <- ...` and handle the `:analytics_down` fallback via an `else` clause that only catches that specific atom (not a complex-else). Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Profiles do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Requires balancing the 'avoid complex else' rule with the need for specific-atom handling.
